what_it_does: Stores a posted noticeboard note (with YAML frontmatter) that includes a screenshot attachment and a comment by Tim on the dragnet-development channel; the body presents a hierarchical document-node tree example and asks for upgrades to markdown and extended markdown rendering.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-ca59cd39, type note, source claude_code, state posted, author_session tim, channel dragnet-development.
Links include commented_on board://item-e62c2915 and an image attachment image://dragnet-development/comment/item-ca59cd39.
History shows filed by tim and an in-place links edit with an empty by field.

Body contains a fenced code block showing a tree of document nodes (doc:report, section:overview, heading, paragraph, sentence, word, list, list-item, zone) linked by part_of relationships, with inline human annotations.

Following the code block, Tim writes that the frontend is new, needs upgrades for markdown rendering and extended markdown rendering, and that extended markdown will help both him and agents writing into the system.
standouts: The body embeds a fine-grained semantic document tree with nodes down to the word and zone level.
The note combines a technical model snippet with informal frontend feedback and a screenshot.
The attachment is an image referenced by a channel-scoped image:// URI.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// and image:// URI schemes for cross-item references.
Uses a Markdown code fence to display an ASCII tree diagram with custom node labels and part_of edges.
concerns: The history entry from edit to links with an empty by field records an edit with no author, making provenance unclear.
The request for markdown rendering and extended markdown rendering upgrades is vague and lacks specific examples or acceptance criteria.
The relationship between the embedded node tree and the screenshot/feedback is not explained in the file.
notes: Tim posted a screenshot note on dragnet-development that illustrates a hierarchical document-node model and asks for better markdown rendering support.
questions: What specific markdown/extended-markdown features are being requested?
How does the attached screenshot relate to the node tree in the body?
What is the intended consumer or semantics of the doc:report node hierarchy?
Why was the links edit recorded with an empty author?
purpose_vs_actual: Labeled as a note/comment and it does contain a comment, but the body is mostly a technical document-tree example while the actual ask is a separate frontend improvement request, so purpose and content only partially align.
intention_signals: Tim appears to be demonstrating a desired document representation (fine-grained nodes with part_of relationships and inline comments) while simultaneously flagging that the current frontend's markdown rendering is too limited to display or support such rich agent-authored content.
novelty: The file mixes an ad-hoc ASCII semantic document tree (down to word and zone nodes) with a casual frontend feature request and a screenshot in a single noticeboard item.
contribution: It preserves a concrete example of a hierarchical document model and a user's stated need for extended markdown rendering, which a fused system could keep as a use case and a model illustration.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim on dragnet-development with a screenshot attachment, containing a hierarchical document-node tree example and a request for markdown and extended markdown rendering upgrades.
intention_for_embedding: To comment on a document model and request frontend improvements for markdown and extended markdown rendering.
